mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling, the discount rate applied to future earnings declines and equity valuations expand, so S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days are positive. The signal is falsified if rate cuts do not coincide with above-baseline forward returns.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS declines over a 1- to 3-month window but the subsequent 20-day SPX return is negative in a majority of triggered cases, or walk-forward Rank IC stays below 0.03, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE above recent levels signals labor-market deterioration and weaker household income growth, which suppresses equity prices over the next month. The effect should be strongest when unemployment is still rising from an expansionary baseline.
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE-triggered periods produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 40% of cases, or if the average forward return is not lower than the unconditional sample at conventional significance.

mechanism_narrative: Rising RSAFS indicates improving retail demand and stronger near-term corporate revenue, so the S&P 500 should post positive returns over the next 5 trading days. The effect is a short-horizon confirmation of demand momentum rather than a valuation story.
failure_condition: Reject if month-over-month gains in RSAFS are not followed by positive 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the 5-day Rank IC is consistently near zero over rolling 18-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST rises while GDPC1 is still recovering, the combination signals a housing-led demand rebound that improves cyclical earnings expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The mechanism fails if housing starts rise without broad output improvement.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising HOUST and improving GDPC1 does not produce above-median 60-day SPX returns, or if either series alone explains the signal as well as the pair.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in INDPRO signals factory output stabilization and inventory liquidation ending, which should lift cyclical equity prices over the next month. The claim is false if industrial production improvements do not translate into positive market returns during the recovery window.
failure_condition: If positive changes in INDPRO are not associated with positive 20-day SPX returns in the majority of recovery episodes, or if the out-of-sample IC is below 0.03 for 2 consecutive years, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A rate cut signal becomes bullish only when CPIAUCSL inflation is not accelerating, because easier policy then lowers real financing costs without immediately reviving inflation fears. In that regime, S&P 500 returns should be positive over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls while CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation is flat or declining
failure_condition: Reject if rate cuts accompanied by flat or falling CPIAUCSL do not outperform other periods on 60-day SPX returns, or if the pairwise interaction adds no predictive value beyond FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE indicates broad labor-market healing, which supports consumer spending and earnings expectations and should lift equities over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism fails if payroll gains are offset by unemployment deterioration.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE do not show positive 20-day SPX returns more often than the unconditional rate, or if reversing either leg removes the signal entirely.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and HOUST both accelerate after a contraction, the economy is shifting from stabilization to broad demand expansion, which should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The hypothesis is false if consumer sales improve without housing confirmation.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both rise above their prior 3-month averages
failure_condition: Reject if the joint-upturn condition in RSAFS and HOUST fails to generate above-median 60-day SPX returns, or if the effect disappears when conditioning on only one of the two series.
